findings.description: ElaroDeliveryQueueable (200 lines) is a Queueable with HTTP callouts that has no dedicated test class and is not referenced in any other test class. This is a production class with zero test coverage.
findings.recommendation: Create ElaroDeliveryQueueableTest.cls with HttpCalloutMock implementation. Test the execute() method, the Finalizer retry logic, and error paths.

findings.description: SlackIntegrationQueueable (361 lines) is the largest untested class. It handles all async Slack operations via HTTP callouts and has no dedicated test class. Not referenced in any other test class.
findings.recommendation: Create SlackIntegrationQueueableTest.cls with HttpCalloutMock for each Slack operation type. Test all enum-dispatched operations, error handling, and the SlackFinalizer.

findings.description: JiraIntegrationQueueable (118 lines) makes HTTP callouts to Jira REST API but has no dedicated test class and is not referenced in any other test class.
findings.recommendation: Create JiraIntegrationQueueableTest.cls with HttpCalloutMock. Test successful issue creation, API error responses, and the JiraIssueFinalizer retry logic.

findings.description: MultiOrgManagerQueueable (228 lines) makes HTTP callouts for multi-org sync and connectivity testing but has no dedicated test class and is not referenced in any other test class.
findings.recommendation: Create MultiOrgManagerQueueableTest.cls with HttpCalloutMock. Test both sync and connectivity operations, and the Finalizer error handling.

findings.description: SlackNotifier.cls makes HTTP callouts (line 124: new Http().send(req)) but SlackNotifierTest.cls (110 lines) does not use HttpCalloutMock or Test.setMock. Tests only verify no exceptions are thrown, which means callout tests would fail in a real test context.
findings.recommendation: Add an inner HttpCalloutMock class to SlackNotifierTest and use Test.setMock() before invoking methods that trigger the SlackBulkNotificationQueueable callout.

findings.description: ElaroIntegrationTest is the only test class (1 of 198) that does not use the @IsTest(testFor) annotation. As an integration test covering multiple classes, it should list its primary target class to support RunRelevantTests (Spring '26).
findings.recommendation: Add @IsTest(testFor=ComplianceServiceFactory.class) or the most appropriate primary production class to the annotation.

findings.description: Only 1 of 21 controller test classes (ElaroAISettingsControllerTest) uses System.runAs() for permission testing. Controllers are entry points with 'with sharing' enforcement, so their tests should verify behavior under different permission sets. Affected: AIGovernanceControllerTest, ApiUsageDashboardControllerTest, AssessmentWizardControllerTest, AuditReportControllerTest, CommandCenterControllerTest, ComplianceDashboardControllerTest, ComplianceScoreCardControllerTest, ElaroDashboardControllerTest, ElaroDrillDownControllerTest, ElaroDynamicReportControllerTest, ElaroExecutiveKPIControllerTest, ElaroMatrixControllerTest, ElaroPDFControllerTest, ElaroTrendControllerTest, EscalationPathControllerTest, OnCallScheduleControllerTest, RuleEngineControllerTest, SECDisclosureControllerTest, TrustCenterControllerTest, TrustCenterGuestControllerTest.
findings.recommendation: Add System.runAs() tests with both admin and standard user profiles using ElaroTestUserFactory. Verify that users without permission sets receive appropriate AuraHandledException errors.

findings.description: 37 of 96 production classes that throw AuraHandledException (39%) have test classes that do not verify the exception paths. This means error handling code is untested, risking unclear error messages reaching users. Key gaps include: AIAuditTrailScanner, AIDetectionEngine, AILicenseDetector, ComplianceDashboardController, IncidentTimelineService, SOC2IncidentResponseService, and others.
findings.recommendation: Add @IsTest methods that deliberately trigger each AuraHandledException path (e.g., null inputs, missing permissions, invalid data). Catch the exception and Assert.areEqual the user-friendly message.

findings.description: Overall assertion message rate is 61% (2664 of 4307 assertions have descriptive messages). While acceptable, several classes are significantly below standard: ElaroAlertTriggerTest (0%), BusinessDayCalculatorTest (30%), ElaroShieldServiceTest (38%), ElaroAuditTrailPollerTest (50%), IncidentTimelineServiceTest (50%), ElaroEventParserTest (56%).
findings.recommendation: Add descriptive message strings to all Assert calls. Messages should explain the expected behavior, not just repeat the assertion (e.g., 'Score should be between 0-100 for valid input' not 'score matches').

findings.description: Health Check package assertion message rate is only 31% (64 of 204 assertions have messages). This is significantly below the main package rate of 61% and far below the recommended 100%.
findings.recommendation: Add descriptive messages to all 140 assertions missing them across the 13 Health Check test classes.

findings.description: None of the 13 Health Check test classes use @TestSetup for shared test data creation. While some HC tests are pure unit tests (like MFAComplianceScannerTest), classes like HealthCheckControllerTest and HealthCheckScannerTest could benefit from shared mock data setup.
findings.recommendation: Add @TestSetup methods to HealthCheckControllerTest, HealthCheckScannerTest, and AuditTrailScannerTest to reduce redundant data creation.

findings.description: ElaroConstantsTest is the only test class (1 of 198) that does not use Test.startTest()/Test.stopTest(). While the class tests pure constants and methods with no governor limit concerns, the pattern should be consistent.
findings.recommendation: Add Test.startTest()/Test.stopTest() wrappers around the method-testing assertions (testGetSeverityFromRiskScore, testGetRatingFromScore, etc.) for consistency.

findings.description: 65 test classes have 4 or more test methods but do not use @TestSetup. This means test data is recreated for each method, increasing test execution time. 93 of 198 (47%) use @TestSetup; 65 with multiple methods could benefit from it.
findings.recommendation: Add @TestSetup methods to test classes with shared data needs. Prioritize classes with the most test methods and DML in each test.

findings.description: MFAComplianceScannerTest has 0 Test.startTest()/Test.stopTest() calls. While the tests are pure calculation methods, adding governor limit boundaries is a best practice.
findings.recommendation: Wrap each @IsTest method body with Test.startTest()/Test.stopTest() for consistency.

findings.description: None of the 13 Health Check test classes use System.runAs(). HealthCheckController is a user-facing entry point and should verify permission behavior.
findings.recommendation: Add System.runAs() tests to HealthCheckControllerTest to verify behavior for users with and without the Health Check permission set.

findings.description: ElaroAlertTriggerTest has 3 assertions but 0% have descriptive messages (2 of 3 areEqual calls lack the message parameter). Only the bulk test has a message.
findings.recommendation: Add descriptive messages: Assert.areEqual('Test Alert', insertedAlert.Title__c, 'Inserted alert title should match').

findings.description: SlackNotifierTest primarily asserts that no exceptions are thrown (Assert.areEqual(null, caughtException, ...)) without verifying actual behavior. This pattern provides coverage but not meaningful validation.
findings.recommendation: Where possible, verify actual outcomes (e.g., that a queueable was enqueued, or that specific log entries were created) rather than just checking for no exception.

findings.description: Main package: 198 test classes for 207 production classes (95.7% coverage ratio). 9 production classes lack dedicated tests, but 5 are interfaces (IAccessControlService, IBreachNotificationService, IDataSubjectService, IEvidenceCollectionService, IRiskScoringService), 1 is an abstract Step interface, 3 are test utilities (ComplianceTestDataFactory, ElaroTestDataFactory, ElaroTestUserFactory), and 1 is a mock (ApiLimitsCalloutMock). The only production classes that genuinely need tests are the 4 Queueable classes (TEST-001 through TEST-004) and ElaroSchedulerTests is itself a test class miscounted as production.
findings.recommendation: Create tests for the 4 Queueable classes. Interfaces and test utilities do not need dedicated tests.

findings.description: Health Check package: 13 test classes for 13 production classes (100% coverage ratio). All 13 test classes use @IsTest(testFor). All classes making callouts (ToolingApiService, HealthCheckScanner, HealthCheckController, SessionSettingsScanner) have HttpCalloutMock implementations.
findings.recommendation: Health Check test coverage structure is complete. Focus on assertion quality (TEST-010) and @TestSetup adoption (TEST-011).

findings.description: 197 of 198 test classes (99.5%) use @IsTest(testFor). All 13 Health Check test classes (100%) use it. Only ElaroIntegrationTest is missing it. This is excellent adoption of the Spring '26 RunRelevantTests annotation.
findings.recommendation: No action needed beyond TEST-006.

findings.description: 197 of 198 main test classes (99.5%) use Test.startTest()/Test.stopTest(). Only ElaroConstantsTest omits it, which is acceptable for a pure constants test class.
findings.recommendation: Excellent adoption. No critical action needed.

findings.description: 72 test classes contain 200-record patterns and 50 use loop-based bulk data creation. Bulk testing is well-represented across the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding bulk tests to controller test classes that accept list inputs but currently only test with single records.

findings.description: All 58 main package LWC components have Jest tests (100%). All 6 Health Check LWC components have Jest tests (100%). Total: 64 of 64 components covered. Shared test utilities (a11yTestUtils.js, axeTestHelper.js, wireAdapterTestUtils.js, setupTests.js) are properly housed in lwc/__tests__/.
findings.recommendation: LWC test coverage is complete. No missing component tests.

findings.description: 24 test classes implement HttpCalloutMock. Of 19 production classes making HTTP callouts, 15 have proper mock implementations in their tests. Gaps: ElaroDeliveryQueueable (no test), SlackIntegrationQueueable (no test), SlackNotifier (test exists but no mock), ElaroEventMonitoringService (callout is commented out, not an issue).
findings.recommendation: Address the 3 callout mock gaps in TEST-001, TEST-002, and TEST-005.
